}

So, the function will be something like:

bool canWin(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desired, int sum_available) {
    if (currentSum >= desired) return true;
    if (currentSum + sum_available < desired) return false;

    // Check memo
    int key = (currentSum << 20) | mask;
    if (memo.find(key) != memo.end()) {
        return memo[key];
    }

    for (int i = 1; i <= max; ++i) {
        int bit = i - 1;
        if (mask & (1 << bit)) {
            int newSum = currentSum + i;
            int newMask = mask & ~(1 << bit);
            int new_sum_available = sum_available - i;
            if (!canWin(newSum, newMask, max, desired, new_sum_available)) {
                memo[key] = true;
                return true;
            }
        }
    }

    memo[key] = false;
    return false;
}

So, the code would look like:

class Solution {
public:
    bool canIWin(int maxChoosableInteger, int desiredTotal) {
        if (desiredTotal == 0) return true;
        int S = maxChoosableInteger * (maxChoosableInteger + 1) / 2;
        if (S < desiredTotal) return false;
        
        int mask = (1 << maxChoosableInteger) - 1;
        unordered_map<int, bool> memo;
        return canWin(0, mask, maxChoosableInteger, desiredTotal, S, memo);
    }
    
private:
    bool canWin(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desired, int sum_available, unordered_map<int, bool>& memo) {
        if (currentSum >= desired) return true;
        if (currentSum + sum_available < desired) return false;
        
        int key = (currentSum << 20) | mask;
        if (memo.find(key) != memo.end()) {
            return memo[key];
        }
        
        for (int i = 1; i <= max; ++i) {
            int bit = i - 1;
            if (mask & (1 << bit)) {
                int newSum = currentSum + i;
                int newMask = mask & ~(1 << bit);
                int new_sum_available = sum_available - i;
                if (!canWin(newSum, newMask, max, desired, new_sum_available, memo)) {
                    memo[key] = true;
                    return true;
                }
            }
        }
        
        memo[key] = false;
        return false;
    }
};

bool canWin(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desired, unordered_map<int, bool>& memo) {
    if (currentSum >= desired) return true;
    
    // Compute sum_available
    int sum_available = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < max; ++i) {
        if (mask & (1 << i)) {
            sum_available += (i + 1);

bool canWin(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desired, int sum_available) {
    if (currentSum >= desired) return true;
    if (currentSum + sum_available < desired) return false;

    // Check memo
    int key = (currentSum << 20) | mask;
    if (memo.find(key) != memo.end()) {
        return memo[key];
    }

    for (int i = 1; i <= max; ++i) {
        int bit = i - 1;
        if (mask & (1 << bit)) {
            int newSum = currentSum + i;
            int newMask = mask & ~(1 << bit);
            int new_sum_available = sum_available - i;
            if (!canWin(newSum, newMask, max, desired, new_sum_available)) {
                memo[key] = true;
                return true;
            }
        }
    }

    memo[key] = false;
    return false;
}

So, the code would look like:

class Solution {
public:
    bool canIWin(int maxChoosableInteger, int desiredTotal) {
        if (desiredTotal == 0) return true;
        int S = maxChoosableInteger * (maxChoosableInteger + 1) / 2;
        if (S < desiredTotal) return false;
        
        int mask = (1 << maxChoosableInteger) - 1;
        unordered_map<int, bool> memo;
        return canWin(0, mask, maxChoosableInteger, desiredTotal, S, memo);
    }
    
private:
    bool canWin(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desired, int sum_available, unordered_map<int, bool>& memo) {
        if (currentSum >= desired) return true;
        if (currentSum + sum_available < desired) return false;
        
        int key = (currentSum << 20) | mask;
        if (memo.find(key) != memo.end()) {
            return memo[key];
        }
        
        for (int i = 1; i <= max; ++i) {
            int bit = i - 1;
            if (mask & (1 << bit)) {
                int newSum = currentSum + i;
                int newMask = mask & ~(1 << bit);
                int new_sum_available = sum_available - i;
                if (!canWin(newSum, newMask, max, desired, new_sum_available, memo)) {
                    memo[key] = true;
                    return true;
                }
            }

        memo[key] = false;

bool canWin(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desired, unordered_map<int, bool>& memo) {
    if (currentSum >= desired) return true;
    
    // Compute sum_available
    int sum_available = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < max; ++i) {
        if (mask & (1 << i)) {
            sum_available += (i + 1);
        }
    }
    
    if (currentSum + sum_available < desired) return false;
    
    int key = (currentSum << 20) | mask;
    if (memo.find(key) != memo.end()) {
        return memo[key];
    }
    
    for (int i = 0; i < max; ++i) {
        if (mask & (1 << i)) {
            int num = i + 1;
            int newSum = currentSum + num;
            int newMask = mask & ~(1 << i);
            if (!canWin(newSum, newMask, max, desired, memo)) {
                memo[key] = true;
                return true;
            }
        }
    }
    
    memo[key] = false;
    return false;
}

This way, the sum_available is computed each time, but it's manageable.

bool canIWin(int maxChoosableInteger, int desiredTotal) {
    if (desiredTotal == 0) return true;
    int S = maxChoosableInteger * (maxChoosableInteger + 1) / 2;

    }
    
    int mask = (1 << maxChoosableInteger) - 1;
    unordered_map<int, bool> memo;
    return canWin(0, mask, maxChoosableInteger, desiredTotal, sum_lookup, memo);
}
